mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in real-time retail sales indicates resilient household demand and supports near-term earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS August 2023 vintage value was 697557.0 as of the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month RSAFS growth do not have higher 20-day forward S&P 500 returns than periods with negative growth in at least 60% of walk-forward yearly tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5% and consumer prices continue rising over the prior 6 months, restrictive policy raises discount rates faster than nominal earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0% while CPIAUCSL 6-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals weakening labor income and recession-risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if drawdown frequency is not higher than baseline after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts despite a high federal funds rate identifies demand strength that survives tight financing conditions, supporting cyclicals and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 10% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
regime_hint: high-rate expansion with resilient housing demand
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST rebound and high-rate interaction has a negative average 60-day S&P 500 return or fails to outperform high-rate periods without a HOUST rebound.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate weakening output momentum and falling operating leverage, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed the full-sample average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth indicates broad demand expansion and higher aggregate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when the latest available GDPC1 trend is rising.
failure_condition: Reject if rising real-time GDPC1 observations fail to deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than falling GDPC1 observations in at least two-thirds of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A reacceleration in consumer prices above a 4% 3-month annualized pace forces tighter rate expectations and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPI reacceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time or if the median triggered return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains combined with a stable unemployment rate signal labor-income growth without recessionary stress, supporting consumption and earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds 300 thousand while UNRATE changes less than 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS strength and stable UNRATE condition does not outperform unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns or if its hit rate is below the sample base positive rate.
